task_completion.description: Open and edit the docker-compose.yml file in the project directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) is open showing the contents of 'docker-compose.yml' located in '/home/ga/projects/payment-gateway'.

task_completion.description: Add a healthcheck configuration to the gateway service.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The docker-compose.yml file contains a 'healthcheck:' block under the gateway service, configured to test 'http://localhost:8000/health' (e.g., using curl).

task_completion.description: Implement a watchdog/autoheal pattern in the docker-compose stack.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The docker-compose.yml file includes a mechanism for auto-healing, such as adding an 'autoheal' service (e.g., using the 'willfarrell/autoheal' image mapping the docker socket) and adding an 'autoheal=true' label to the gateway service, or a custom script service that monitors and restarts the gateway.

task_completion.description: Apply the changes by recreating or updating the Docker stack.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows a command like 'docker compose up -d' or 'docker-compose up -d' being executed, with output indicating the containers are being recreated or started.

task_completion.description: Trigger the deadlock bug using the sabotage endpoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the exact command 'curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/sabotage' being executed.

task_completion.description: Verify that the service automatically recovers after being sabotaged.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows evidence of automatic recovery, such as 'docker ps' showing a very recent 'Up' time for the gateway container, 'docker events' showing a container restart, or a subsequent successful 'curl http://localhost:8000/health' command returning a healthy status without any manual restart commands being issued.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not manually restart the container to fake the auto-healing process.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal history does NOT show commands like 'docker restart', 'docker compose restart', or 'docker stop' followed by 'docker start' after the sabotage command was executed.

integrity.description: Ensure the sabotage command was actually executed and not just typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/sabotage' command is followed by a new terminal prompt or server response, proving the command was submitted.

integrity.description: Ensure the modifications to docker-compose.yml were saved before applying.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor shows no 'unsaved changes' indicators, or a terminal command like 'cat docker-compose.yml' confirms the healthcheck and watchdog configurations are present in the saved file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project directory is /home/ga/projects/payment-gateway. The gateway service runs on port 8000. The health endpoint is http://localhost:8000/health (returns HTTP 200 when healthy, HTTP 500 when unhealthy). The sabotage endpoint is http://localhost:8000/sabotage and requires a POST request. A successful autoheal implementation will detect the unhealthy status (HTTP 500) after sabotage and automatically restart the container within a 60-second window.
